content: Hi, I'm Devon, an AI ethics researcher at a think tank in San Francisco. I've been dealing with chronic back pain lately, and it's been really tough.
I'm so sorry to hear that. Chronic back pain can be really debilitating. Have you tried any new treatments or therapies recently?
Yeah, I've been trying a few different things. I've started doing some urban sketching to help me relax, and I've also been going to physical therapy to try and strengthen my back.
Urban sketching is a great way to unwind. Do you have a favorite place to sketch in the city?

I love sketching in the Mission District. There's always something interesting to draw, and the people-watching is great. I've also been trying to get back into marathon running, which I used to love, but it's been tough with my back pain.
Marathon running is a big commitment. Have you considered modifying your training to make it more low-impact, or taking a break from running altogether?

I've been thinking about it, but I'm not sure what to do. I've been running for years, and it's hard to give it up. I've also been hiking with my friend, Rachel, which has been helpful for my back, but also frustrating because I want to be able to do more.

It can be tough to adjust to new limitations. But it's great that you have a supportive friend like Rachel to hike with. Have you considered talking to a doctor or a sports medicine specialist about your back pain and how to manage it?

Yeah, I've been to see a few different doctors, and I'm still trying to figure out the best course of treatment. It's frustrating, but I'm trying to stay positive and focus on the things I can still do, like hiking and sketching.
That's a great attitude to have. And it's good that you're prioritizing your health and taking care of yourself. Do you have any fun plans or trips coming up that you're looking forward to?
Actually, I'm planning a trip to Yellowstone with my family next month. We're going to spend a week hiking and exploring the park, which I'm really looking forward to.
